heading: Funding for OPIC programs
label: 5828 Funding for OPIC programs
notes: References in TextThe Foreign Assistance Act of 1961, referred to in subsec. (a), is , , . Chapter 11 of part I of the Act is classified generally to part II (§ 2295 et seq.) of subchapter I of chapter 32 of this title. Title IV of chapter 2 of part I of the Act, which was classified generally to subpart IV (§ 2191 et seq.) of part II of subchapter I of chapter 32 of this title, was repealed, except for subsections (g), (), (m), and (n) of , by , , , effective at the end of the transition period, as defined in . For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 87–19575 Stat. 424section 2197 of this titlePub. L. 115–254, div. F, title VI, § 1464(2)132 Stat. 3513section 9681 of this titlesection 2151 of this titleSept. 4, 1961Oct. 5, 2018lThe Overseas Private Investment Corporation Act Amendments Act of 1992, referred to in subsec. (b), probably means the Overseas Private Investment Corporation Amendments Act of 1992, which was title I of H.R. 4996, 102d Congress, as passed by the House of Representatives. H.R. 4996 was enacted into law as and title I was significantly revised and no longer contained provisions designating it as the Overseas Private Investment Corporation Amendments Act of 1992.Pub. L. 102–549

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of functions, personnel, assets, and liabilities of the Overseas Private Investment Corporation to the United States International Development Finance Corporation and treatment of related references, see sections 9683 and 9686(d) of this title.

children.heading: Authority to make additional funds available
children.label: a Authority to make additional funds available
children.text: Funds authorized to be appropriated for fiscal year 1993 to carry out chapter 11 of part I of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 [ et seq.] may be made available to cover costs incurred by the Overseas Private Investment Corporation in carrying out programs with respect to the independent states of the former Soviet Union under title IV of chapter 2 of part I of that Act ( and following), in addition to amounts otherwise available for that purpose.22 U.S.C. 229522 U.S.C. 21911See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Enactment of OPIC Authorization Act
children.label: b Enactment of OPIC Authorization Act
children.text: The authority of subsection (a) shall cease to be effective upon the enactment of the Overseas Private Investment Corporation Act Amendments Act of 1992.
